steps.title: Identify the decisive clue
steps.content: The passage says Seacole faced "every conceivable obstacle" crossing the Isthmus of Panama. The word "every" signals the full range of obstacles that could be thought of or imagined.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
"Conceivable" needs to mean able to be imagined. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Visible: able to be seen with the eyes — the passage doesn't say the obstacles were visible, but that they could be conceived of mentally. ✗
  • Choice B — Steady: constant or unwavering — this describes persistence, not the range of obstacles one can think of. ✗
  • Choice C — Easy: not difficult — this contradicts the passage, which calls the journey not easy. ✗
  • Choice D — Possible: able to exist or be imagined — "every possible obstacle" captures the full range Seacole could think of, matching "conceivable." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
